data.title: Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ: TNDM) reports Q4 earnings of 1 cent per share with sales at $290.400 million, surpassing estimates. FY2026 sales expected at $1.065-1.085 billion. CEO expresses positive outlook.
data.description: In its recent report, Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM) exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter financial performance. The company recorded a quarterly loss of 1 cent per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of 8 cents per share in losses. Additionally, Tandem Diabetes Care achieved quarterly sales of $290.4 million, outperforming the analyst consensus of $277.029 million in sales. Looking forward, Tandem Diabetes Care anticipates FY2026 sales between $1.065 billion and $1.085 billion, contrasting with market estimates of $1.103 billion. President and CEO John Sheridan expressed satisfaction with the achievements in 2025, highlighting surpassing $1 billion in global sales, setting gross margin records, and enhancing commercial operations and business models. The company aims to continue this success in 2026 by strengthening its operations.

data.title: Oceaneering to participate in investor conferences on February 26, March 3, and March 17, 2026.
data.description: Houston-based company Oceaneering has disclosed its upcoming participation in several investor conferences. The company will be present at conferences scheduled for February 26, March 3, and March 17. These events provide opportunities for Oceaneering to engage with potential investors and showcase its operations and financial outlook. This initiative aims to strengthen its investor relations and potentially attract more investment into the company.

data.title: Nexon appoints Patrick Söderlund as Executive Chairman to accelerate global strategy, with 3659.TO stock symbol.
data.description: A major player in online gaming, NEXON Co., Ltd. (3659.TO) headquartered in Tokyo, declared Patrick Söderlund as the Executive Chairman. Söderlund, known for his strategic and creative acumen, will spearhead Nexon's transformation with immediate effect. This leadership move empowers Söderlund to steer the long-term strategy and creative direction of the company.

data.title: Western Union stock dropped due to mixed Q4 results. Earnings at 45 cents per share beat estimates, but revenue missed at $1.008 billion.
data.description: On Friday, Western Union Co (NYSE: WU) shares are plummeting due to the company's recent quarterly results and fiscal year 2026 guidance. Despite surpassing analyst predictions with fourth-quarter earnings at 45 cents per share, a 12.5% increase from the previous year, the revenue fell short at $1.008 billion, a 4.73% decrease year-over-year compared to the expected $1.045 billion. The company forecasts GAAP sales between $4.254 billion to $4.375 billion and adjusted sales between $4.237 billion to $4.357 billion, exceeding the consensus estimate of $4.120 billion. The adjusted EPS guidance, ranging from $1.75 to $1.85, is near or slightly above expectations. This has led to bearish pressure on Western Union stock as investors react to the mixed results and guidance provided by the company.

data.title: Marimekko Corporation bought back shares on Feb 20, 2026.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to just 3.8%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. This decrease in unemployment is particularly notable given that just last year, the rate was at 5.5%. The number of new jobs added to the economy also saw a significant increase, with 300,000 jobs created in the past month alone. This surge in job creation has had a positive impact on various sectors, including manufacturing, technology, and healthcare.Experts attribute this impressive job growth to a combination of factors, including strong consumer spending, business investments, and government policies aimed at boosting economic growth. The report also indicates that wages have started to rise, with the average hourly wage increasing by 4% compared to last year. Overall, the outlook for the job market appears optimistic, with many predicting continued growth and expansion in the coming months.

data.title: Capitol Securities congratulates Sean O'Sullivan on his new role within the firm after starting his investment career in late 2024.
data.description: In a statement released in Washington on February 20, 2026, Capitol Securities expressed their congratulations to Sean O'Sullivan for his recent appointment within the company. Sean joined the investment firm in late 2024 as a Wealth Management Associate, where he honed his skills in client service, trading, compliance, and marketing. The announcement marks a significant milestone in Sean's career trajectory within the financial sector.

data.title: Inventor from Commerce Township creates KEY RING CABINET to organize garage items like keys.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 20, 2026, an inventor from Commerce Township, Michigan, shared the inspiration behind the creation of the KEY RING CABINET. The inventor expressed the desire to develop a unique wooden cabinet aimed at organizing keys, sunglasses, and other items within a garage while keeping them tidy and secure. The innovative design of the KEY RING CABINET effectively addresses this need by eliminating clutter and providing a structured storage solution.

data.title: Invisors ranks #4 in 2026 Best Places to Work list in South Florida for medium companies - 4th consecutive year.
data.description: In a recent announcement made on February 20, 2026, the South Florida Business Journal revealed that Invisors, a Workday Services Partner, has achieved the impressive ranking of 4th place on the 2026 Best Places to Work list in the medium company category. This recognition marks Invisors' fourth consecutive appearance on the list, showcasing their dedication to providing a positive work environment.

data.title: Abbott announces 409th consecutive quarterly dividend of 63 cents/share, paid since 1924, payable on May 15, 2026.
data.description: Abbott headquarters in Abbott Park, Illinois announced on February 20, 2026, that the board of directors has approved a quarterly common dividend of 63 cents per share. This dividend payment of 63 cents per share will be the 409th consecutive quarterly dividend to be paid by Abbott since the year 1924. Shareholders can anticipate to receive this cash dividend on May 15, 2026. Abbott is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ABT.

data.title: RECX debuts 23" TerraTread™ for MAX 2, largest OEM tire; 25" model for 2026 expansion & MAX 4/Buffalo Truck.
data.description: A groundbreaking development in the tire industry has taken place with the introduction of the 23x11-8 TerraTread tire, now the largest OEM tire available for the MAX 2 model. In addition to this, a variant sized at 25x12-10 has been released, setting the stage for the relaunch of the MAX 4 and Buffalo Truck models. These new tire sizes mark significant progress in the industry and point towards exciting prospects for the future.
